Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth

Class BearerTokenGuard

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.RestGuard
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.BearerTokenGuard
public class BearerTokenGuard extends RestGuard
AuthN RestGuard that authenticates requests via the RFC 6750 Bearer Token scheme.

On every request, the guard:

  1. Reads the Authorization request header.
  2. Confirms it starts with "Bearer " (case-insensitive) followed by a non-blank token.
  3. Delegates the raw token to the configured TokenValidator.
  4. On success, stashes the returned Principal on the request attributes under RestServerConstants.PRINCIPAL_ATTR so downstream handlers can read it via @Auth Principal or req.getAttributes().get(PRINCIPAL_ATTR).
  5. On any failure path (missing header, malformed scheme, validator throws), throws AuthenticationException with a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="<realm>" response header set per RFC 7235 §4.1.
Usage

@Rest(path="/api") public class ApiResource extends RestServlet { @Bean(name="guards") public RestGuardList guards(BeanStore bs) { return RestGuardList.create(bs) .append( BearerTokenGuard.create() .realm("api") .validator(myTokenValidator) .build()) .build(); } }

Security notes
  • Bearer tokens are credentials — never log the full token, never echo it in a response body, and always pair with TLS in production.
  • TokenValidators should run in constant time when possible (in particular, opaque-token lookups should use constant-time comparison to defeat timing side-channels).
  • For JWT verification, use the JwtTokenValidator shipped in the optional juneau-rest-server-auth-jwt sub-module — it enforces algorithm allowlisting and mandatory iss / aud / exp / nbf checks by default.
See Also:
  • TokenValidator
  • AuthenticationException
  • Auth
  • RFC 6750 — Bearer Token Usage
  • AuthN Guards
Since:
10.0.0

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static BearerTokenGuard.Builder create()

      Returns:
      A new builder.

    • guard

      public boolean guard(RestRequest req, RestResponse res)
      Description copied from class: RestGuard
      Checks the current HTTP request and throws a BasicHttpException if the guard does not permit the request.

      By default, throws an SC_FORBIDDEN exception if RestGuard.isRequestAllowed(RestRequest) returns false.

      Subclasses are free to override this method to tailor the behavior of how to handle unauthorized requests.

      Overrides:
      guard in class RestGuard
      Parameters:
      req - The servlet request.
      res - The servlet response.
      Returns:
      true if request can proceed. Specify false if you're doing something like a redirection to a login page.

    • isRequestAllowed

      public boolean isRequestAllowed(RestRequest req)
      Description copied from class: RestGuard
      Returns true if the specified request can pass through this guard.
      Specified by:
      isRequestAllowed in class RestGuard
      Parameters:
      req - The servlet request.
      Returns:
      true if the specified request can pass through this guard.
